assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md classifies as 'CODE CHANGE REQUEST' with action 'Sub-task created to address this feedback.' subtask-30001.md exists in the outputs directory confirming sub-task creation. report.md Review Comment Classifications table: '30001 | Code Change Request | Sub-task created'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies as 'SUGGESTION'. Reasoning explicitly identifies: (1) '"should also add" -- the phrase "should also" is a characteristic suggestion marker', (2) '"would help" -- hedging language indicating the reviewer sees this as beneficial but not strictly required', (3) '"Something like:" -- this phrase explicitly frames the SQL as an example or starting point'. All three suggestive language markers are cited and explained.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md classifies as 'NIT'. Action: 'No sub-task created. Nit-level feedback does not affect correctness and is left to the PR author's discretion.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in outputs directory (confirmed by directory listing).

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md classifies as 'QUESTION'. Action: 'No sub-task created. Questions ask for clarification and do not require code changes.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in outputs directory (confirmed by directory listing).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, value 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13, 5 bullet points), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20, 4 checkboxes). Each section has substantive content.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md line 29: '## Target PR' followed by line 30: 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. Exact URL match confirmed.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 32) with Comment ID: 30001, Author: reviewer-a, File: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (line 60), and the full original comment text reproduced verbatim on line 36.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains exactly 6 files (confirmed by ls): report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md. All are markdown analysis/report files. No source code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) were created or modified.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments have corresponding classification files: review-30001.md through review-30004.md. report.md Review Feedback row references all 4: '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping); 1 suggestion (comment 30002: index on deleted_at); 1 nit (comment 30003: context message wording); 1 question (comment 30004: GET behavior for deleted SBOMs)'. The Review Comment Classifications table lists all 4 with classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md overall result is 'FAIL' (line 17). No mention of merge, auto-merge, or any merge action anywhere in report.md or any other output file. The report concludes with findings, sub-task information, and root-cause investigation only.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file with 5 integration tests). No existing test files modified or deleted. |' Detailed findings section (lines 109-111) confirms: 'Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE' with explanation that tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file not present on base branch.

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full 'Convention Upgrade Evaluation' subsection (lines 23-33) checking 3 sources: (1) CONVENTIONS.md -- 'not available for content analysis', (2) Codebase pattern check -- 'PR diff does not contain evidence of established index creation patterns', (3) Performance-related scrutiny -- 'No documented performance conventions found'. Upgrade decision: 'No upgrade.' report.md lines 81-88 'Convention Upgrade -- PASS' section also evaluates comment 30002 and reaches the same conclusion.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: No subtask-30002.md file exists in outputs directory (confirmed by ls: only subtask-30001.md present). review-30002.md Action: 'No sub-task created. The suggestion remains classified as a suggestion -- an optional performance improvement.' Convention upgrade decision: 'No upgrade. The suggestion does not match any documented convention.' report.md table: '30002 | Suggestion | No sub-task (no convention match for upgrade)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found on the PR.' Detailed findings (lines 105-107): 'Eval Quality -- N/A / No eval result reviews found on the PR (no reviews from github-actions[bot] with `## Eval Results` marker and `sdlc-workflow/run-evals` footer).' All 3 detection criteria are explicitly named. Test Quality row shows PASS with Eval Quality as N/A component.
